TFDA Post-Market Quality Management System
sources.manual_extract: Official TFDA post-market quality management portal for cosmetics and related regulated products. Use this source when a Taiwan cosmetics review needs to confirm adverse-event reporting route, post-market surveillance workflow, defective product handling, recall linkage, serious adverse effect escalation, complaint handling, or responsible-firm post-market obligations. The portal is operational and may require interactive browser access, account login, or form navigation, so LabelPass stores this structured operational extract as the reusable evidence anchor while retaining the live official URL for verification. Pair it with TFDA adverse-event reporting guidance and the MOJ serious adverse reporting regulation before making a final dossier decision. Practical review workflow: identify the product name, product notification or registration context, responsible firm, importer, manufacturer, lot or batch number, complaint date, event date, event narrative, injury or hygiene/safety hazard description, corrective action, recall or withdrawal status, reporter contact, follow-up record, and whether the event meets serious adverse effect reporting thresholds. Search and routing aliases include QMS, post-market quality management, adverse event, serious adverse effect, cosmetics complaint, defective cosmetic, recall, product safety complaint, 衛生安全危害, 嚴重不良反應, 化粧品不良事件, 產品回收, 품질관리, 이상사례, 부작용, 회수, 사후관리, and 대만 화장품 안전 보고. In label or claim screening, flag this source when files mention consumer injury, irritation, allergy, infection, product defect, contamination, microbial issue, safety complaint, recall trigger, or medical consultation. If the portal cannot be reached automatically, record the attempted search keyword, account or form route, visible screenshot, and companion legal source used. If product identity, lot, responsible firm, or event severity cannot be matched, route the review to expert follow-up and do not treat marketing safety claims as substantiated.

TFDA Food and Drug Business Registration Platform
sources.manual_extract: Official TFDA Food and Drug Business Registration Platform, commonly referenced as the fadenbook portal and 非登不可. Treat this as an operational companion source for Taiwan food, drug, and cosmetics business registration, operator account workflows, product notification, product registration, filing support, and responsible-firm routing. The portal is interactive and may not expose complete searchable text to automated HTTP clients, so LabelPass stores this manual operational extract as the stable knowledge anchor while the live official URL remains the verification authority. For cosmetics review, use it with TFDA cosmetic product registration platform evidence, product notification rules, PIF management evidence, GMP or manufacturer documents, and importer/responsible-firm data. Practical workflow: confirm whether the Taiwan responsible firm or importer has the right portal route, identify the product family, product name, cosmetic category, notification or registration obligation, PIF readiness, manufacturer or factory data, business registration context, account holder, submission status, and any mismatch between label owner, importer, and responsible firm. Search aliases include fadenbook, 非登不可, Food and Drug Business Registration Platform, product notification, cosmetic filing, product registration, responsible firm, importer account, TFDA portal, 化粧品產品登錄, 化粧品登錄, 業者登錄, 食品藥物業者登錄平台, 수입자 등록, 책임업체, 제품등록, 제품 신고, PIF 제출, and 대만 화장품 등록. In a LabelPass review, this source should trigger when the label file mentions Taiwan importer, responsible person, product notification number, product registration category, PIF file, GMP evidence, manufacturer record, or account-based TFDA submission. If the product cannot be tied to the responsible firm, require updated importer data before approving Taiwan label release.

sources.manual_extract: Official TFDA Consumer Knowledge Service Network page for Food Additive Permit Data Query. The live URL is a dynamic official lookup page and may return generic resource text to automated HTTP clients, so LabelPass stores this manual extract as the searchable evidence anchor while retaining the official URL for verification. Use this source when a label, product specification, import file, supplier declaration, or product registration document mentions a food additive permit, food additive registration, food additive license number, permit number, registration number, inspection registration, 登錄碼, 許可證字號, 登記證, 食品添加物許可證, 食品添加物登錄碼, 食品添加物查驗登記, 食品添加物許可證字號, 식품첨가물 허가 조회, 식품첨가물 허가번호, or 식품첨가물 등록번호. The practical workflow is to search the TFDA permit data by additive or product name and permit-style identifiers, confirm whether the additive product appears in the TFDA permit dataset, and reconcile the result with the food additive standards, inspection-registration materials, and registration announcements before approving Taiwan import or label wording. This source is a database routing and evidence source, not a substitute for checking the applicable additive scope, specification, application, limitation, and product-category conditions.

sources.title: Enforcement Rules of Health Food Control Act
Health Food Permit Data Query
sources.authority: Taiwan Food and Drug Administration / Consumer Knowledge Service Network
sources.manual_extract: Official TFDA Consumer Knowledge Service Network page for health food permit lookup. Use it to confirm health food permit numbers, product names, applicant or permit holder context, registration status, approved health-care effect scope, standard health-food logo eligibility, and whether a product may lawfully present itself as health food in Taiwan. The live page is a database lookup and can be shallow or dynamic under automated collection, so LabelPass keeps this structured manual extract as the reusable search anchor while retaining the official URL for browser verification. Practical workflow: search by permit number, license number, product name, applicant name, brand name, functional ingredient, or Chinese health-food wording; capture the result record; compare approved effect scope with the label claim; verify permit number display, recommended intake, warning statement, target population, functional ingredient, dosage or serving instruction, expiration context, and whether supporting import documents match the product. Search aliases include health food permit, health food license, registration number, permit number, 保健食品, 健康食品, 健康食品許可證, 衛部健食字, 健康食品查詢, approved health-care effect, function claim, health food logo, 허가번호, 건강식품 허가, 보건효능, 기능성 문구, 대만 건강식품, and TFDA permit query. In review workflows, open this lookup before accepting claims about immunity, cholesterol, gastrointestinal function, blood sugar, liver protection, fatigue, bone health, body fat, dental health, or any approved health-care effect. If the product cannot be found, the permit number does not match, or the approved effect scope is narrower than the label wording, route the case to claim substantiation and false or exaggerated advertising review instead of ordinary food labeling approval.

sources.title: Formula for Certain Disease labeling requirements
Special Disease Formula Food Permit Data Query
sources.authority: Taiwan Food and Drug Administration / Consumer Knowledge Service Network
sources.manual_extract: Official TFDA Consumer Knowledge Service Network page for special disease formula food permit lookup. Use it to verify permit numbers, product names, applicant or permit holder context, registered formula type, disease or medical nutrition category, registration status, and whether the Taiwan label, certificate, or import file can rely on a specific approved formula record. The page is a database lookup and may not produce stable full text through automated collection, so LabelPass stores this operational extract as the reusable knowledge anchor while the live official URL remains the authority for browser verification. Practical workflow: search by permit number, license number, product name, applicant name, formula type, disease-specific nutrition term, or Chinese product wording; capture the visible result; compare formula category, intended use, patient-use instruction, professional supervision wording, nutrition support claim, import document product identity, and label warnings against the approved record. Search aliases include special disease formula food, formula for certain disease, special medical purpose food, special dietary food, patient formula, disease formula, 特定疾病配方食品, 特殊營養食品, 特定疾病配方食品許可, 許可證字號, special food permit, permit query, 특수의료용도식품, 특수영양식품, 질환별 조제식품, 환자용 식품, 허가조회, and 대만 특수질환식품. In screening, trigger this source when labels mention diabetes, renal disease, dysphagia, tube feeding, cancer nutrition, inborn metabolism, low protein, enteral nutrition, complete nutrition, medical use, hospital use, physician supervision, or disease-specific dietary management. If the product, permit number, or disease category cannot be matched, require additional registration evidence and route the case away from ordinary food label approval.

Illegal Food, Drug, and Cosmetics Advertising Query System
sources.authority: Taiwan Food and Drug Administration / PMDS
sources.manual_extract: Official TFDA / PMDS illegal food, drug, and cosmetics advertising query system for enforcement lookup. Use this as a companion source when screening Taiwan food or cosmetics label claims, health-food effects, medical-efficacy wording, false or exaggerated advertising, therapeutic implication, before-after claims, and missing substantiation. The system is operational rather than explanatory: reviewers should use it to compare risky wording against violation records and enforcement examples for food, drug, and cosmetics advertising. In LabelPass, route claim-heavy products here when the label or marketing draft mentions disease treatment, body-function improvement, slimming, whitening, anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, detox, immune, medical efficacy, or approved health-care effects. The review workflow should record the search keyword, product category, brand or product name, wording under review, and whether a similar violation appears in the query result. For Taiwan food, pair this lookup with false, exaggerated, misleading, or medical-efficacy claim rules, nutrition-claim guidance, sweetness-claim guidance, health-food permit checks, and special disease formula permit checks. For Taiwan cosmetics, pair it with cosmetic labeling and claims criteria, product notification, PIF substantiation, restricted ingredient evidence, and adverse-event or recall routing when safety claims are involved. The source should trigger a conservative result when marketing copy implies treatment, prevention, diagnosis, cure, body-structure change, disease-specific benefit, guaranteed effect, or government approval beyond the actual permit scope. It should also be used during Korean-to-Traditional-Chinese claim translation review, because the same Korean ingredient or efficacy wording may map to multiple Taiwan enforcement terms. Store search keywords and screenshots in the review file when a close precedent is found, then link the result back to the product claim, evidence document, and final label decision. If the portal is not reachable by automated collection, keep this manual record as the stable knowledge anchor and refresh by browser capture when the site is available.

sources.title: Import and Export Administration
Import and Export Regulations by CCC Code
sources.manual_extract: Official Taiwan International Trade Administration/MOEA database page for Classification of Commodities and Regulations. The English interface is titled Import and Export Regulations and supports searching by commodity code, commodity code range, or imported file. It accepts HS code inputs from 2 to 6 digits and CCC code inputs under the Standard Classification of Commodities of the Republic of China up to 10 digits, with examples including 01, 2001, 740729, 0203120000, and 2207101010. The page also supports commodity-description keyword search, such as fish, bovine animals, or chemical, and regulation-code search for import or export regulation types. The form refers to Import Regulation codes and Export Regulation codes, with examples including numeric codes and alphanumeric codes such as 111, 401, 272, C01, and MW0. The page provides download areas for import/export regulation information and an Importation of Mainland China products section. It explains that where EX is appended after a CCC number, only goods conforming to the corresponding Chinese and English commodity description are opened for import. It also notes that where Mainland China product import special regulation codes such as MXX or NXX are listed, the goods must satisfy both general import regulations and those special provisions before import is allowed. The user workflow is to identify a commodity by HS or CCC number, confirm whether an import regulation code, export regulation code, permit, inspection, or agency restriction appears, and then map the result back to the responsible agency and shipment documents. This makes the page a primary routing source before preparing invoices, packing lists, origin evidence, TFDA documents, SHTC checks, or customs entries. This source is used by LabelPass for CCC-code based routing, import/export permit signals, trade-control screening, and search terms such as CCC code, HS code, import regulation code, export regulation code, regulation codes, commodity description, controlled goods, permit code, regulatory agency, and trade controls. It is a query database rather than a static legal text, so LabelPass stores this manual extract to preserve the official page semantics for search and review evidence while the live URL remains the authority.

sources.title: Commodity Classification and Import/Export Regulation Guide
GC453 Tariff Database Download
sources.manual_extract: Official CPT Single Window / Customs Administration page GC453 Tariff Database Download. The page is a browser-oriented official download page for Taiwan tariff and CCC-code reference data. It identifies the CCC CODE last modification date and presents official download options for tariff and commodity-code work. Listed resources include Tariff Schedule files in spreadsheet, PDF, and open-document formats; CCC Code and Description of Goods files in text and PDF formats; chapter-by-chapter tariff materials; Application for Import Goods Advance Tariff Rulings files in document, PDF, and open-document formats; and Tariff No. and Description of Goods files in text and PDF formats. The page is relevant when a product team needs to confirm the Taiwan customs statistical classification, compare HS candidates, download the current tariff schedule, or prepare an advance classification ruling before import. The tariff schedule and CCC goods-description files support screening for duty rate, import/export controls, TFDA routing, BSMI routing, SHTC routing, and other agency rules that depend on commodity classification. Advance tariff ruling materials are important when product composition, use, kit status, or mixed-material goods make the final CCC code uncertain. LabelPass uses this source to connect HS code and Taiwan CCC code searches with tariff classification, customs statistical code review, duty-rate routing, and import/export regulatory screening. Practical search terms mapped to this source include GC453, tariff database download, tariff schedule, CCC CODE, CCC code description of goods, tariff number, tariff no., customs tariff, HS code, Standard Classification of Commodities of the Republic of China, advance tariff ruling application, import goods advance tariff ruling, 관세율표, 대만 CCC 코드, 세번, 稅則號列, 貨品分類號列, 海關編碼, 進口貨物稅則預先審核, tariff no. and description of goods, and customs import tariff. Automated HTTP access may return a browser-only message, so LabelPass keeps this manual extract from the official page while retaining the live official URL for audit and refresh.

What is the Harmonized System
Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals Rev. 11
sources.authority: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe
sources.manual_extract: Official UNECE index for the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals, eleventh revised edition, 2025. The source page routes to the free electronic edition and to language downloads including the English PDF file GHS Rev11e.pdf. Official document citation: ST/SG/AC.10/30/Rev.11. The GHS, also known as the Purple Book, is the global baseline used by many jurisdictions for chemical hazard classification and hazard communication. It supports regulatory checks for substance and mixture classification, physical hazards, health hazards, environmental hazards, signal words, hazard pictograms, hazard statements, precautionary statements, product identifiers, supplier identification, label elements, and safety data sheet terminology. Rev. 11 takes account of amendments adopted by the UN Committee of Experts on the Transport of Dangerous Goods and on the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals in December 2024. Search anchors for this source include GHS, UN GHS, Purple Book, Globally Harmonized System, chemical classification, labelling of chemicals, SDS, safety data sheet, hazard communication, CLP alignment, OSHA HazCom alignment, pictograms, signal word, Danger, Warning, H statements, P statements, aerosols, chemicals under pressure, skin sensitization, non-animal methods, global warming potential, simple asphyxiants, Annex 11, precautionary statement rationalization, and ST/SG/AC.10/30/Rev.11. Additional retrieval anchors include classification criteria, flammable gases, flammable aerosols, flammable liquids, oxidizing gases, oxidizing liquids, oxidizing solids, explosives, self-reactive substances, pyrophoric liquids, pyrophoric solids, self-heating substances, water-reactive substances, organic peroxides, corrosive to metals, desensitized explosives, acute toxicity, skin corrosion, serious eye damage, respiratory sensitization, germ cell mutagenicity, carcinogenicity, reproductive toxicity, specific target organ toxicity single exposure, specific target organ toxicity repeated exposure, aspiration hazard, hazardous to the aquatic environment, hazardous to the ozone layer, and hazardous by contributing to global warming. This cached text is an official-source routing and retrieval note for environments where direct automated HTTP capture requires an interactive browser challenge; the legal reference remains the UNECE page and PDF.

sources.title: GHS Rev. 11 English PDF
sources.authority: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe
sources.manual_extract: Official UNECE English PDF for the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals, eleventh revised edition, 2025. File name: GHS Rev11e.pdf. Official document citation: ST/SG/AC.10/30/Rev.11. Publication metadata identifies it as a United Nations publication issued by the United Nations Economic Commission for Europe, with ISBN 978-92-1-003273-5 and PDF ISBN 978-92-1-003274-2. The English PDF is the authoritative source to retrieve when the system needs detailed evidence for GHS chemical classification and labelling rules. It should be used for chemical product review, hazard class identification, category terminology, signal word checks, pictogram checks, hazard statement checks, precautionary statement checks, supplier and product identifier checks, and safety data sheet terminology. Rev. 11 includes update anchors for aerosols and chemicals under pressure, skin sensitization classification using non-animal methods, substances and mixtures that are hazardous by contributing to global warming, rationalized precautionary statements, and Annex 11 guidance on simple asphyxiants. Operational aliases include UN GHS Rev.11, GHS 2025, Purple Book 2025, ST/SG/AC.10/30/Rev.11, hazard communication, chemical labelling, SDS, safety data sheet, pictogram, signal word, H statement, P statement, acute toxicity, skin corrosion, eye damage, carcinogenicity, reproductive toxicity, aquatic toxicity, ozone layer, global warming, aerosol, chemical under pressure, and simple asphyxiant. Detailed search anchors include label element, product identifier, supplier identification, supplemental information, hazard class, hazard category, mixture classification, bridging principle, cut-off value, concentration limit, precautionary pictogram, transport pictogram, skin sensitizer, respiratory sensitizer, mutagenicity, aspiration toxicity, metal corrosion, oxidizer, flammable solid, self-heating, pyrophoric, organic peroxide, desensitized explosive, combustible dust, and safety data sheet section terminology. This cached text is a retrieval note and search anchor for the official PDF in environments where direct automated PDF capture requires an interactive browser challenge; the legal reference remains the UNECE PDF URL.
